prompt:
Our wool co-op's broker toolkit just got pulled into the org-wide build, and the org build runs javac with -Xlint:all -Werror. The intake registry module now refuses to compile. It's old code from before we used generics properly, but it is CORRECT old code — the lot groupings, pen totals and the heaviest-first report all match the shed books, and TestMain pins all of it. Here's a taste of the wall of output:

    BaleRegistry.java:15: warning: [rawtypes] found raw type: Map
    BaleRegistry.java:17: warning: [rawtypes] found raw type: List
    BaleRegistry.java:21: warning: [rawtypes] found raw type: Comparator
    BaleRegistry.java:35: warning: [unchecked] unchecked call to put(K,V) as a member of the raw type Map
    BaleRegistry.java:48: warning: [unchecked] unchecked cast
    BaleRegistry.java:57: warning: [unchecked] unchecked method invocation: method sort in class Collections is applied to given types
    BaleRegistry.java:58: warning: [unchecked] unchecked conversion
    BaleRegistry.java:62: warning: [rawtypes] found raw type: List
    BaleRegistry.java:71: warning: [unchecked] Possible heap pollution from parameterized vararg type List<Bale>
    TestMain.java:59: warning: [unchecked] unchecked generic array creation for varargs parameter of type List<Bale>[]
    error: warnings found and -Werror specified

I want this done as a proper generics retrofit, not a warning mute: our lint policy bans @SuppressWarnings outright, so type the thing correctly instead. Callers keep their shapes — TestMain builds the truck manifest by passing several pens straight into loadOrder(...) and that call style has to keep working, warning-free. No behavior changes anywhere: every grouping, total, ordering and tag the tests pin stays exactly as it is. Verification is `bash run_verify.sh` (strict build then the suite); don't touch TestMain.java, run_verify.sh or .gitignore.
notes: 5W4 item 1: -Xlint:all -Werror generics retrofit. Authoring toolchain: openjdk/javac 26.0.1 (brew). Verify is bash run_verify.sh: javac -Xlint:all -Werror -d .build *.java (in-memory source launcher cannot carry the lint gate) then java -cp .build TestMain; .build/ is gitignored so no .class pollution. Planted, all compile-clean without -Werror so semantics are well-defined pre-fix: raw-type declaration sites x5 (Map/HashMap field, List/ArrayList log field, raw Comparator with raw anonymous class, raw local pen in intake, raw List param on totalKg), unchecked casts/conversions ((List<Bale>) from Object in lot(), unchecked conversion returning raw List as List<Bale> in heaviestFirst plus unchecked Collections.sort, unchecked ArrayList ctor in intakeTags), and one generic-varargs heap-pollution declaration (loadOrder(List<Bale>...)) which also produces unchecked generic-array-creation warnings at the THREE frozen TestMain call sites — so a signature change away from varargs breaks the frozen tests and the intended fix is @SafeVarargs on the static method (prompt bans only @SuppressWarnings; discovering @SafeVarargs is part of the lesson). Reference: generify fields/locals/params, Comparator.comparingDouble().reversed().thenComparing(), computeIfAbsent, @SafeVarargs. Behavior teeth: lot grouping + intake order, defensive copy from lot(), exact double pen totals (binary-clean .25/.5/.75 weights), heaviest-first with equal-weight tie broken by ticket id (W042/W045 both 187.5), manifest flattening incl. zero-pen and one-pen varargs calls, weighbridge tag sequence. Domain check: no existing seed covers wool/shearing intake; nearest java generics seed is java-typedbus (build-events, dispatcher design) — this is a retrofit of working raw-type code, different kind and layer.
